_schema.purpose: wq-089 — disclosures feeding the Compute Ledger. Each component decomposes its AI revenue into three buckets: (1) frontier-lab compute, (2) non-frontier workload compute (AI Native + enterprise + model-serving cos), (3) token-API resale (Bedrock + AOAI + Vertex partner-served). Pass-through % applies to bucket 3 only. Copilot-class revenue is scoped OUT and tracked separately. derive_compute_revenue.py reads this file and writes site-data.json:compute.* . Refresh quarterly within ~48h of each major hyperscaler earnings.
_schema.tiers: 1A = direct quotation from filed 10-K/10-Q. 1B = press release / earnings call narrative (own-mouth). 2A = editorial estimate with documented methodology (e.g., AI portion of total Cloud, bucket decomposition). 2B = inferred from analyst consensus.
_schema.principal_agent_status: ALL FOUR (MSFT/AMZN/GOOGL/ORCL) confirmed PRINCIPAL (gross) treatment per data/methodology_constants.json:compute_revenue.principal_agent_treatment, verified 2026-05-06 from 10-K accounting policy notes. The 'principal-everywhere' assumption in wq-087 §5.2 holds — gross sums are valid; bucket-3 net requires editorial pass-through estimation.
_schema.bucket_model: Bucket 1 (frontier_lab_compute): per-lab revenue × cloud-mix; the circular-financing line. Bucket 2 (non_frontier_workload_compute): residual after bucket 1 + Copilot deduction; AI-native customers + enterprise model-serving cos buying GPU hours / managed inference. Bucket 3 (token_api): resale of frontier models via Bedrock / Azure OpenAI Service / Vertex partner-served; pass-through % applies here. See methodology.html §Compute Ledger — three-bucket model.
_schema.copilot_scope_out: Copilot-class revenue (M365 Copilot, GitHub Copilot, Copilot Studio for MSFT; Gemini-in-Workspace for GOOGL; equivalent embedded-AI-feature lines for AMZN/ORCL where identifiable) is per-seat productivity SaaS, not Compute. Scoped OUT of every aggregate published on /compute.html. Tracked-but-excluded; flows to a future Apps Ledger when that is built. Per-component figure surfaces in copilot_excluded_*_usd_b for transparency.
_schema._caveat: All AI-attributable revenue lines for hyperscalers are editorial Tier 2A unless explicitly disclosed. MSFT discloses AI run-rate (Tier 1B). AMZN/GOOGL disclose 'multi-billion AI revenue' / 'gen AI revenue grew 800%' narrative-only — quantum is editorial. ORCL OCI AI portion is editorial. Refresh and tighten each cycle as disclosure granularity improves.

msft_ai.label: Microsoft Azure AI
msft_ai.bucket_basis: Bucket 1 (frontier-lab compute, $20.0B 2025): OpenAI is ~85% Azure-resident; OpenAI 2025 calendar revenue ~$12.5B + equity-funded burn implies $18–22B compute spend on Azure (cash + prepaid commits). Bucket 3 ($1.5B 2025): Azure OpenAI Service to enterprise customers — small slice of MSFT AI line at realistic token spend. Pass-through 20% (Zitron-leaked rev-share methodology). Bucket 2 ($1.5B): residual non-frontier enterprise + AI-Native workload on Azure. Copilot excluded $5.0B: M365 Copilot ~$3B + GitHub Copilot ~$1.2B + Copilot Studio ~$0.8B (per-seat productivity SaaS, scoped to future Apps Ledger). Tie-out: 20+1.5+1.5+5.0 = $28.0B = original AI run-rate.

ai_run_rate_disclosure.source_quote: Microsoft's AI business surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $37 billion, up 123% year-over-year.
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Microsoft FY26 Q3 earnings press release / call commentary

msft_ai.notes: Bucket-decomposed per wq-089. The disclosed $28B 2025 annualised AI line decomposes 20.0/1.5/1.5/5.0 across buckets 1/2/3 + Copilot. Bucket 1 dominates because OpenAI's compute spend on Azure is the central circular-financing line on the page.

googl_cloud_ai.label: Google Cloud AI
googl_cloud_ai.bucket_basis: Bucket 1 (frontier-lab compute, $11.5B 2025): Anthropic on GCP ~$3B (split of Anthropic's ~$10B 2025 compute spend, ~30% GCP); Mistral, Cohere, smaller-lab partners on Vertex ~$3B; the rest is Google's own equity-cycle spend (Google funds DeepMind / Gemini development through GCP — the internal recursive line that surfaces externally as 'GCP AI revenue'). Bucket 3 ($0.5B): Vertex partner-model resale; lower than Bedrock because Anthropic-on-Vertex partnership is newer and smaller. Pass-through 20%. Bucket 2 ($3.0B): non-frontier enterprise AI on GCP. Copilot excluded $3.0B: Gemini-in-Workspace + Gemini for Google Cloud SKUs — per-seat productivity, scoped to future Apps Ledger. Tie-out: 11.5+3.0+0.5+3.0 = $18.0B = 2A AI-attributable estimate (30% of GCP cloud).

ai_run_rate_disclosure.narrative_quote: revenue from products built on Google's gen AI models grew nearly 800% year-over-year
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Alphabet Q1 2026 earnings call commentary; AI portion of Cloud is editorial 2A estimate

googl_cloud_ai.notes: Bucket-decomposed per wq-089. Bucket 1 includes Google's own equity-cycle compute spend supporting DeepMind/Gemini development as a frontier-lab line at the segment level — this is editorially defensible because Google's internal model R&D consumes GCP capacity and books as GCP AI revenue. If Cowork pushes back, alternative is to scope this out under a 'first-party-internal' line. Flagged as potential decision.

amzn_aws_ai.label: AWS AI
amzn_aws_ai.bucket_basis: Bucket 1 (frontier-lab compute, $13.0B 2025): Anthropic 2025 ~$4.71B revenue runs ~70% on AWS (per AWS-Anthropic strategic partnership disclosures); Anthropic compute spend ~$10B (cash + AWS credits + prepaid commits) × 70% = ~$7B AWS-resident. Plus other frontier labs on AWS (Mistral via Bedrock partner, Cohere on AWS, AI21, smaller labs) ~$3B. Plus Bedrock infrastructure layer for partners ~$3B. Bucket 3 ($2.5B): Bedrock customer-paid resale of frontier models (Anthropic primarily, plus Llama/Mistral). Pass-through 25% blended (Anthropic-on-Bedrock has different terms than AOAI). Bucket 2 ($2.5B): non-frontier enterprise + AI-Native workload on AWS. Copilot excluded $0: AWS does not have a Copilot-equivalent named productivity line — embedded AI features (Q Developer, Connect AI) too small to attribute separately at 2A; default 0 per brief edge case. Tie-out: 13+2.5+2.5+0 = $18.0B.

ai_run_rate_disclosure.narrative_quote: AWS's AI business is generating multi-billion-dollar annualized revenue and growing at triple-digit percentages year over year. Bedrock client spending +170% Q-over-Q in Q1 2026.
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Amazon Q1 2026 earnings call (April 29, 2026); narrative-only — quantum is editorial

amzn_aws_ai.notes: Bucket-decomposed per wq-089. The Anthropic-AWS partnership ($4B initial + $4B follow-on commitment) is the single largest line in bucket 1. AWS-OpenAI Q1 2026 announcement of $38B + $100B/8yr expansion (per 10-Q Note 'Commitments and Contingencies') is RPO not realised revenue and does not affect 2025 buckets — will start surfacing in bucket 1 from 2026 onwards as billings begin.

oracle_cloud_ai.label: Oracle OCI AI
oracle_cloud_ai.bucket_basis: Bucket 1 (frontier-lab compute, $1.0B 2025): OpenAI Stargate workload begins billing through OCI as data centers come online late 2025; partial-year contribution. The contracted $300B OpenAI commitment is RPO not realised revenue (per wq-087 brief §8.5). Bucket 3 ($0): Oracle does not run a token-API resale comparable to Bedrock/AOAI/Vertex. Bucket 2 ($2.0B): general OCI AI workload — enterprise AI Native + research / model-serving cos. Copilot excluded $0: Oracle has no Copilot-class line. Tie-out: 1+2+0+0 = $3.0B. Update each cycle as Stargate billings ramp (bucket 1 will grow rapidly through 2026).

ai_run_rate_disclosure.narrative_quote: AI-attributable portion of OCI; Oracle does not separately disclose AI revenue. OCI growth narrative driven by 'unprecedented AI demand' and RPO disclosure of $300B+ OpenAI/Stargate commitment.
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Oracle Q3 FY26 earnings call commentary; AI-attributable line is editorial 2A estimate

underlying_segment.note: Cloud revenue includes both OCI and SaaS (Fusion + NetSuite). OCI is the AI-relevant subset; AI portion of OCI is editorial.

oracle_cloud_ai.notes: ORCL fiscal year ends May; Q3 FY26 = Dec 2025 - Feb 2026 quarter. Tighten as Stargate revenue ramps through 2026.

coreweave.label: CoreWeave
coreweave.bucket_basis: Bucket 1 (frontier-lab compute, $3.8B 2025): ~85% of CoreWeave revenue is Microsoft sub-rent — much of which is OpenAI workload Microsoft sub-rents to CoreWeave to handle peak frontier-lab demand. This is the 'circular financing made visible' line on the Layer Stack: OpenAI pays Azure → Azure pays CoreWeave → both surface as compute revenue at the ecosystem level. Bucket 2 ($0.7B): other AI Native + research customers on CoreWeave directly. No bucket 3 — pure-play GPU cloud, no token-API resale. Tie-out: 3.8+0.7+0+0 = $4.5B = S-1 / 10-Q disclosed.
coreweave.notes: CoreWeave IPO'd March 2025. The Microsoft-sub-rent-for-OpenAI line is editorially the most important callout for the Layer Stack — it's where the circular-financing geometry is most visible to a non-specialist reader.

nebius.label: Nebius
nebius.bucket_basis: Bucket 1 (frontier-lab compute, $0.6B 2025): Mistral and other smaller frontier labs renting capacity from Nebius (US-Europe footprint). Bucket 2 ($0.35B): AI Native customers + research. No bucket 3. Tie-out: 0.6+0.35+0+0 = $0.95B.
nebius.notes: Nebius (NBIS) — formerly Yandex N.V. assets, US-listed AI cloud. 6-K filer. Bucket 1 / bucket 2 split is editorial estimate; tighten if Nebius discloses customer concentration.

lambda.label: Lambda Labs
lambda.bucket_basis: Bucket 1 (frontier-lab compute, $0.4B 2025): smaller frontier labs + research orgs renting GPU capacity from Lambda. Bucket 2 ($0.2B): AI Native customers + sole-trader / academic. No bucket 3. Tie-out: 0.4+0.2+0+0 = $0.6B.
lambda.notes: Private. Press-disclosed run rate; tighten if Lambda files S-1. Bucket split is 2A editorial.

crusoe.label: Crusoe
crusoe.bucket_basis: Bucket 1 (frontier-lab compute, $0.25B 2025): smaller labs + applied-research customers renting GPU capacity. Bucket 2 ($0.15B): AI Native + general research. No bucket 3. Tie-out: 0.25+0.15+0+0 = $0.4B.
crusoe.notes: Private. Press-disclosed. Bucket split is 2A editorial.

yoy_reference._doc: 2024 sum-of-quarterlies for the YoY-growth headline (Box 4 on hero strip per wq-089 D4). Editorial Tier 2A — anchored against MSFT FY24 AI exit-run-rate disclosures (~$13B), AWS 'multi-billion' narrative early 2024, GCP AI estimate ~25% of cloud, Oracle / neocloud aggregates pre-Stargate. Refresh when audited 10-Ks publish a tighter 2024 number.
yoy_reference.compute_revenue_2024_gross_source: Editorial 2A reconstruction from 2024 hyperscaler AI disclosures (MSFT ~$13B exit run-rate per FY24 Q4 commentary, AMZN multi-billion narrative, GCP ~$8B implied at 25% AI-share-of-cloud, ORCL ~$1B, neoclouds ~$3B aggregate). Methodology mirrors 2025 bucket build but with 2024 cohort sizes.

layer_stack_inputs._doc: Inputs for the Layer-Stack visual (per wq-089 D3). All four layers on lookback 2025 actuals on the same time-basis (sum-of-quarterlies for compute, NVDA Data Center FY26 calendar 2025 for silicon, Power Ledger placeholder for power, live measured-cohort apps revenue from sankey for apps). Apps Revenue is published as the cohort total — explicitly not editorially extended to ecosystem scale. Bridge to ecosystem Apps is documented on /methodology.html as a future-Ledger gap, not a number on /compute.html.
layer_stack_inputs.apps_revenue_2025_source: Live read from site-data.json:sankey.totalCustomerRevenue (~$17.36B as of 2026-05-06) — the Revenue Ledger cohort total (Tier 1A-derived). NOT editorially multiplied to ecosystem scale; the cohort-vs-ecosystem gap is flagged on /methodology.html as a future-Apps-Ledger scope.
layer_stack_inputs.compute_revenue_2025_source: Sum of 2025 quarterly grosses minus 2025 Copilot-excluded total. Per wq-089 D3, Layer Stack uses sum-of-quarterlies (not 'annualised hybrid' from the prior schema) so all four layers share a time basis.
layer_stack_inputs.silicon_revenue_2025_source: NVIDIA Data Center revenue FY26 (calendar 2025) ~$135B + AMD MI300/MI350 ~$5B + custom ASIC (Broadcom, Marvell) ~$25B
layer_stack_inputs.power_revenue_2025_source: Editorial — DC power + utilities AI-attributable. Power Ledger placeholder (wq-006).

_provenance.set_by: claude-code (wq-089 — bucket decomposition + Copilot scope-out + pass-through correction)
